title: Adobe Creative Cloud - Logging in with Your UW-Madison Account
body: How do I access Adobe Creative Cloud? The Creative Cloud Desktop app is the best way to download, install, and update your Adobe Creative Cloud apps . Apps included in the UW-Madison ETLA is here: Adobe - Enterprise Term License Agreement (ETLA) . Download Adobe Creative Cloud This installation requires that you have admin privileges on your computer. If you do not have admin privileges on your computer, please contact your IT support team for assistance installing this software. Adobe Creative Cloud See the following article for more information on the downloading process: Adobe Help - Download Creative Cloud App . DoIT Contract Partners can use Departmental Support - BigFix Self-Service Options to install without needing admin rights. How do I sign in to Adobe Creative Cloud with the UW-Madison license? After you download, install and launch the Creative Cloud Desktop app, you'll be presented with the Sign In screen. Type your NetID email (netid@wisc.edu) in the Email address field and click the blue Continue button. Do not use your first.last@wisc.edu email address. Select the orange Company or School account option UW-Madison's NetID login screen will open, and you will log in with your NetID and NetID password. After you finish logging in with your NetID, you'll be returned to the Creative Cloud Desktop app with your available applications displayed. From there, you can install, update, or open the apps. Your Adobe Federated account is completely separate from any other Adobe accounts you may have, even if prior Adobe accounts use your netid@wisc.edu email address . If you do have a prior Adobe account under your netid@wisc.edu email address, you'll be presented with a choice between Personal Account and Company or School Account when signing in. To access your Federated Account, choose Company or School Account . Adobe Creative Cloud storage capacity Always back up your files. Never store the only copy of a file in the cloud storage associated with your Creative Cloud subscription. Please contact your IT support staff for assistance in backing up UW files, or follow this document for advice on backing up your personal files: Computing at UW - Backing Up Your Personal Data . Storage capacity is the 100 GB for UW-Madison licensed users and this cannot be increased. If you need a large amount of Adobe cloud storage, you can look into the individual Photography plan option at this link: Individual pricing plans . IMPORTANT NOTE: This will not integrate with your UW Adobe account and is a separate personal account. The cloud storage is not shared between them. There is no way to increase your UW Adobe cloud storage. Software included in the ETLA Acrobat DC (aka Acrobat Pro) After Effects Animate (the software previously known as Flash Professional) Audition Behance (mobile/web only) Bridge Captivate (separate subscription required through the Campus Software Library) Character Animator Dimension Dreamweaver Fireworks CS6 Flash Builder Premium Fresco Illustrator InCopy InDesign Lightroom Lightroom Classic Media Encoder Muse CC Photoshop Photoshop Express Prelude Premiere Pro Premiere Rush Scout CC Spark Substance 3D Typekit XD Note: Stock, Sign, and Presenter are not included in the ETLA. Tips for troubleshooting common login issues If you get stuck into a NetID authentication loop during the initial install, please do the following: Open a web browser and go to https://creativecloud.adobe.com Log into your UW account on this website Try to log in again through the Creative Cloud app. The Adobe Creative Cloud Desktop app displays a "You don't have access to manage apps" message Resolve other trial and license expired errors Adobe Creative Cloud - All applications in the desktop app are listed as trial versions White or Blank Screen When Installing Creative Cloud with Federated ID on Mac Use the Adobe Creative Cloud Cleaner tool to uninstall and completely remove previous Creative Cloud installations Launching Acrobat on Mac - Error Message Including the Words, “The Serial Number 1591[…] Is Already in Use by the Maximum Allowed Computers” [Link for document 99747 is unavailable at this time] Fix licensing errors for Creative Cloud apps Resolve connection errors How to fix Creative Cloud sign-in loop Activation limit reached Adobe app installation stuck See Also Adobe Creative Cloud - Student Subscription FAQ Eligibility for Adobe Creative Cloud Licensed By University of Wisconsin System's ETLA Adobe Shared Device License - Installation and Usage Instructions Computing at UW - Backing Up Your Personal Data
